ai_difficulty_reason: 18 - trivial 1-step linear solve (x+4=11 -> x=7); (1) obviously sufficient, (2) plainly a range; only DS-format familiarity adds any load.
34 - single-concept DS but must test 33-39 for composites to confirm 37 is the lone prime; (2) 'odd' clearly insufficient, no engineered trap.
50 - parity reasoning on both statements (k^2 even and 3k even each force k even); answer D, and the trap is wrongly ruling one insufficient, tempting A/B.
42 - classic 2-equation DS (p+c=48, c=3p); each alone insufficient, together unique -> C; distractors D/A bait over-crediting a single statement.
58 - (1) trivially reduces to x>y but (2) x^2>y^2 needs the negative counterexample (x=-3,y=1); C/D are engineered traps for solvers who skip sign cases.
46 - set up and solve 2x2 system (x+y=30, 4x+9y=200) via substitution; light arithmetic, two-column select limits guessing but no real trap.
62 - combined-rate solve (480/50=9.6) yielding a non-integer H that contradicts the 'same whole number of hours' red-herring wording; then 30*9.6=288 for column two.
40 - synthesize two sources and apply an AND-condition (before 2 PM AND all in stock); answer D, distractors B/E dangle irrelevant out-of-stock item C not in this order.
